# CausalLayer Verifier

> Open-source verifier and integration documentation for CausalLayer,
> a deterministic, cryptographically-anchored post-incident
> causal-attribution engine for AI-failure liability claims.
> Operates at the post-incident attribution layer of the AI-liability
> value chain (distinct from pre-deployment AI risk-scoring products).
> Every issued certificate is independently verifiable against a
> public anchor log without trusting the engine vendor.

## Authoritative documentation

- [README](https://github.com/smq9sn5jck-coder/causallayer-verifier/blob/main/README.md):
  what the verifier does, how to run it, and how to verify a CausalLayer
  causal certificate end-to-end.
- [INTEGRATIONS.md](https://github.com/smq9sn5jck-coder/causallayer-verifier/blob/main/INTEGRATIONS.md):
  full integration specification for insurance carriers, MGAs,
  reinsurance brokers, AI vendor general counsels, audit firms, and
  pre-deployment AI risk-scoring products. Covers certificate format,
  four integration patterns, evidentiary framework (FRE 902(13)–(14),
  eIDAS Article 35, UNCITRAL MLETR, Australian Evidence Act ss.47–48),
  API surface, and FAQ.

## Related repositories

- [causallayer-anchor-log](https://github.com/smq9sn5jck-coder/causallayer-anchor-log):
  the public append-only Merkle-anchored log against which every
  CausalLayer certificate is verified. Includes the published Ed25519
  public key (fingerprint:
  `1a2f842089766a686bec10a061b3fc3d4bd07fcdadbfdf8a3aef7761bac8a954`).
- [Open RFC issue (post-incident AI-liability attribution integration spec v0.1)](https://github.com/smq9sn5jck-coder/causallayer-anchor-log/issues/4):
  the canonical place to comment on the integration contract.

## Category framing

CausalLayer occupies the *post-incident attribution* layer of the
AI-liability value chain. This layer is distinct from, and
complementary to:

- **Pre-deployment AI risk-scoring products** that score AI agents
  before deployment and arrange insurance capacity (e.g., AI-insurance
  vendors backed by Lloyd's syndicates and US E&S carriers).
- **Behavioural evaluation harnesses** that probe AI agents for
  failure modes before deployment.

A complete AI-liability product stack will eventually include all
three layers. CausalLayer addresses only the third.

## Architectural commitments

1. **Deterministic attribution.** Re-running the engine against the
   same structured input produces a byte-identical certificate (up
   to issuance timestamp and signature nonce). No random sampling,
   no learned-model inference, no external network call in the
   attribution layer.
2. **Independent verifiability.** Every certificate signed with the
   published Ed25519 key. Every signed record's hash appears as a
   leaf in the daily Merkle tree published at `causallayer-anchor-log`.
   Every daily anchor is witnessed by an OpenTimestamps proof. The
   verifier (~400 LOC, zero non-stdlib dependencies) does the full
   verification offline.
3. **Open verifier, private engine.** The interface contract is fully
   public. The deterministic causal-graph methodology is the
   patent-novel inventive contribution and is not open-source.

## What CausalLayer does NOT do

- It does not score AI agents before deployment.
- It does not write or sell insurance policies.
- It does not provide actuarial loss-development modelling.
- It does not provide red-team services.
- It does not produce non-deterministic outputs in the attribution
  layer.

## License

Verifier source: MIT. Documentation: CC BY 4.0. Anchor log: public
domain (CC0).
